category_subtitle: Learn how to count small objects in Japanese using 個
category_detail:
Counting Small Items in Japanese

In Japanese, small objects are often counted with the counter 「個」.

This counter is very common and is used for many everyday things such as apples, eggs, candies, balls, boxes, fruits, and other small separate items.

Basic Pattern
The counter is usually read as こ (ko), but the pronunciation changes with some numbers for easier speaking.

1個 (いっこ / ikko)
2個 (にこ / niko)
3個 (さんこ / sanko)
4個 (よんこ / yonko)
5個 (ごこ / goko)

Important Sound Changes
Some numbers change their sound before 個:
1個 becomes いっこ
6個 becomes ろっこ
8個 becomes はっこ
10個 becomes じゅっこ / じっこ

These small pronunciation changes are very common in Japanese and should be memorized.

item_list.description: The reading changes from いちこ to いっこ for natural pronunciation.
よんこ is the standard and most natural reading for learners.
The reading changes from ろくこ to ろっこ for easier pronunciation.
ななこ is easier and very natural for learners. しちこ may also be heard.
The reading changes from はちこ to はっこ for natural pronunciation.
Both じゅっこ and じっこ are used. じゅっこ is very common in learning materials.
The final part follows the sound pattern of 1個, so it becomes いっこ.

item_list.hiragana: じゅうよんこ / じゅうしこ
item_list.description: じゅうよんこ is more natural and easier for learners. じゅうしこ may also be heard.
The final part follows the sound pattern of 6個, so it becomes ろっこ.

item_list.hiragana: じゅうななこ / じゅうしちこ
item_list.description: じゅうななこ is natural and easy for learners. じゅうしちこ may also be used.
The final part follows the sound pattern of 8個, so it becomes はっこ.

item_list.hiragana: にじゅっこ / にじっこ
item_list.description: Both にじゅっこ and にじっこ are used. The sound changes because of 個.
